name: Animals
description: Creatures great and small from across the animal kingdom

questions.q: What is a baby kangaroo called?

source.notes: Most marsupial babies are called joeys (koalas, wombats, opossums).

questions.q: What is the name for a female peacock?

source.notes: 'Peafowl' is the general term. Males have the ornate train, not females.

questions.q: What is the name for a female fox?

source.notes: Males are called 'dogs' or 'tods.' Babies are 'kits' or 'cubs.'

questions.q: What is the name for a female donkey?

source.notes: Also called a 'Jennet.' Male donkeys are 'Jacks.'

questions.q: What is the fastest land animal in the world?

source.notes: Can reach 70 mph in short bursts. Unique among big cats: can't retract claws fully.

questions.q: What is the color of a polar bear's skin under its fur?

source.notes: Hair is actually translucent, not white. Skin is black to absorb heat.

questions.q: A rhinoceros's horn is made primarily of what substance?

source.notes: Same material as human fingernails and hair. Not bone.

questions.q: Which reptile has the most powerful bite force of any living animal?

source.notes: ~3,700 PSI. Humans bite at ~160 PSI.

questions.q: Which animal has the highest blood pressure of any living creature?

source.notes: ~280/180 mmHg, twice a human's. Needed to pump blood up its ~6 ft neck.

questions.q: What is the only food that koalas eat?

source.notes: Eat ~1 pound of leaves daily. Leaves are toxic to most other animals.

questions.q: What is the largest living species of bird?

source.notes: Can reach 9 feet tall, 330 lbs. Flightless. Native to Africa.

questions.q: The St. Bernard dog breed originated in which European country?

source.notes: Bred by monks at the Great St. Bernard Hospice in the Swiss Alps for Alpine rescue.

questions.q: Primatologist Jane Goodall is most famous for her decades-long study of which species?

source.notes: Began studying at Gombe Stream, Tanzania in 1960. Discovered chimps use tools.

questions.q: What is the largest mammal on Earth?

source.notes: Up to 100 ft long, 200 tons. Likely the largest animal ever to have lived.

questions.q: How many legs does a spider have?

source.notes: Spiders are arachnids, not insects. Insects have 6 legs.

questions.q: The 'killer whale' is more scientifically known by what other name?

source.notes: Orcinus orca. Largest member of the dolphin family, not technically a whale.

questions.q: In seahorses, which parent uniquely carries the developing young?
choices: Neither — they're laid in a nest

source.notes: Females deposit eggs into male's brood pouch. Male gives birth.

questions.q: Seahorses belong to which scientific genus?

source.notes: Also the name of a structure in the human brain shaped like a seahorse.

questions.q: What is the term for a group of whales or dolphins traveling together?

source.notes: Usually 2-30 individuals. Some species form 'superpods' of 1,000+.

questions.q: An omnivore is an animal that eats what?

source.notes: From Latin: omnis (all) + vorare (devour). Humans, bears, pigs are omnivores.

questions.q: What is the scientific term for an animal that only eats plants?

source.notes: From Latin herba (plant). Cows, horses, deer, most rodents.

questions.q: Which continent are tigers native to?

source.notes: Despite cultural associations with Africa, tigers are only wild in Asia.
